Bourcard Nesin, beverage analyst at Rabobank, previously told CNN that the tournament would provide a welcome boost for drinks producers operating in a difficult US market. “The World Cup will increase consumption of beer, wine and spirits without question,” he said. “It's a big boost for them at a time when they need it in a market where they need it most.”

Extended trading hours in the UK

Back in Britain, operators are also preparing for increased demand should the home nations progress through the tournament. Pubs in England and Wales will be permitted to remain open until 1am or 2am for home nation matches from the round of 32 onwards, with many licensees hoping to replicate the strong trading performance seen during England's run to the Euro 2022 final.

title: Gleneagles and Dom Pérignon unveil new private dining experience
summary:
The Gleneagles Hotel has partnered with Dom Pérignon to launch a new private dining and tasting concept centred on Champagne pairings, Scottish produce and rare vintages.

The post Gleneagles and Dom Pérignon unveil new private dining experience appeared first on The Drinks Business.
content:
The Gleneagles Hotel has partnered with Dom Pérignon to launch a new private dining and tasting concept centred on Champagne pairings, Scottish produce and rare vintages.

The Gleneagles Hotel and Dom Pérignon have announced a new partnership that will see the launch of The Cellar x Dom Pérignon, a private dining and tasting experience at the Scottish hotel.

Available to book from 15 June 2026, the experience combines a five-course menu created by Gleneagles' chefs with a selection of Dom Pérignon cuvées. The dining room accommodates up to 12 guests and has been designed to reflect the shared heritage and sense of place of both brands.

Historic cellar transformed

Located behind the scenes at Gleneagles, The Cellar has served as the hotel's storage and ageing space for rare wines and spirits for more than 100 years. According to the hotel, trains once transported wines from London, with bottles lowered by hand into the cellar below.

The space has now been converted into a private dining room while remaining a working cellar. It sits among Gleneagles' collection of Burgundy, Bordeaux and Super Tuscan wines and features bespoke Champagne cabinets designed to store Dom Pérignon at its optimum ageing and serving temperature.

Design details reference Dom Pérignon's home at the Abbey of Hautvillers in Épernay, including a custom-made travertine dining table inspired by the chalk, clay and limestone soils of the Champagne region. The table was delivered by crane in two sections through a service hatch that was last used to unload bottles from delivery trains in the 1920s and 1930s.

Tasting experiences

The signature Dom Pérignon Experience begins with two glasses of Dom Pérignon Vintage 2017 served alongside King's Golden Oscietra Caviar and accompaniments. Guests then enjoy a five-course tasting menu, plus an amuse bouche and pre-dessert, with each course paired with a different Dom Pérignon expression, including Dom Pérignon Vintage 2017, Dom Pérignon Rosé 2010 and Dom Pérignon Vintage 2008 Plénitude 2.

The Cellar also houses a library of more than 40 Dom Pérignon rarities. Guests can book bespoke tastings, including vertical tastings and selections from landmark vintages.

Other experiences available include a Dom Pérignon & Canapé Reception featuring two glasses of Dom Pérignon Vintage 2017 and four canapés per person, a Dom Pérignon Tasting with five Dom Pérignon expressions paired with canapés, and The Cellar Experience, which pairs the tasting menu with wines selected from the Gleneagles wine list.

The Cellar x Dom Pérignon will be available to book through the Gleneagles website from 15 June 2026. Guests must reserve the full dining experience at least three weeks in advance, while tastings can be booked up to three days ahead. Prices start from £150 per person, rising to £695 per person for The Dom Pérignon Experience.

title: ALTA launches Basque-inspired Bar ALTA concept in Soho
summary:
London's ALTA will transform its ground floor into a more casual pintxos-style bar next week, placing greater emphasis on wines by the glass, vermouth and sherry.

The post ALTA launches Basque-inspired Bar ALTA concept in Soho appeared first on The Drinks Business.
content:
London's ALTA will transform its ground floor into a more casual pintxos-style bar next week, placing greater emphasis on wines by the glass, vermouth and sherry.

ALTA will launch a new ground-floor concept, Bar ALTA, on 23 June, introducing a more casual dining experience inspired by the pintxos culture of Northern Spain.

The first-floor restaurant will continue to operate as usual, serving ALTA's original menu of elevated sharing dishes centred around charcoal cooking.

The new concept has been developed by head chef Robbie Jameson, formerly head chef at HUMO, who joined ALTA in May. Drawing on his experience of cooking over fire and his interest in the bar culture of the Basque Country, Jameson has created a menu designed for more spontaneous dining, with both reservations and walk-ins welcomed. The offering will also extend to the restaurant's ground-floor terrace.

Casual dining with a stronger by-the-glass focus

Bar ALTA's menu will feature a selection of smaller dishes and daily specials, including Grilled Flatbread with Green Romesco & Manchego, Spiced Cecina with Kampot Peppercorn Oil, and Padrón Peppers with Mint, Sour Cream & Pickled Chilli.

Larger sharing dishes will include A4 Wagyu with Marmite, Honey & Espelette Pepper and Corn-Fed Chicken with Fermented Chilli & Chicken Crumb.

The drinks programme will continue to focus on savoury, food-led cocktails inspired by Spanish drinking culture. Wine and craft cider will remain central to the offer, with the same wine list shared between Bar ALTA and the upstairs restaurant.

However, Bar ALTA will place greater emphasis on wines by the glass, allowing guests to explore a wider range of styles in a more casual setting. A rotating selection of vermouths and sherries will also be available.

To support the new format, the ground floor is being redesigned with additional high-top and counter seating overlooking the kitchen, while the 20-seat terrace has been refreshed with more high tables and banquette seating.

Bar ALTA will operate from 12pm to 10pm, Tuesday to Saturday, and from 5pm to 10pm on Sundays, aiming to reflect Spain's relaxed aperitif culture by encouraging guests to drop in for drinks and snacks or an informal lunch.

Upstairs restaurant continues

Alongside the launch of Bar ALTA, Jameson will continue to oversee the menu served upstairs, with new summer dishes including Highland Lobster rice, Iberico & smoked cheddar croquettes and Oak smoked Yellowtail & Ajoblanco joining existing dishes such as Txistorra, Squid, Lardo & Vizcaína Sauce, and Chocolate & Olive Oil.

Earlier this year, ALTA was recognised in the Michelin Guide and was nominated for Best New Opening at the National Restaurant Awards.

Artem Login, founder of MAD Restaurants, said: "With Bar ALTA, we wanted to introduce another way to enjoy the restaurant—something more relaxed and spontaneous, and to complement the ALTA people know and love, which will continue to be offered upstairs. Whether it's a couple of small bites and a vermouth on the terrace, or a table full of dishes shared with friends, Bar ALTA is designed to capture the energy and conviviality of the bars you'll find across the Basque Country. Summer is the ideal time to introduce this new offering to Soho and Kingly Court."
